latest.title: Results of AGM
latest.content:
30 Dec 2025 13:19
RNS Number : 1826N
Westminster Group PLC
30 December 2025
30 December 2025
Westminster Group Plc
('Westminster', the 'Group' or the 'Company')
Results of AGM
Westminster Group Plc (AIM: WSG) announces that at the Annual General Meeting held today at 10am Resolutions 1-3 were passed by the requisite majority. Resolutions 4 - 7 were withdrawn from the agenda of the AGM prior to the event following consultation with a number of shareholders.
The proxy votes cast in respect of each resolution are below:
No.
Ordinary Resolutions
For
Against
Votes withheld
1
To re-appoint Mr Peter Fowler as a Director
155,262,914
15,200,189
181,538
2
To re-appoint Mr. Mark Hughes as a Director
155,302,914
15,160,189
181,538
3
To appoint Miss Jing Zhou as a Director
167,714,065
2,760,576
170,000
A resolution "To receive the audited Financial Statements for the financial year ended 30 June 2025" will be put to a General Meeting in the New Year.
The chairman of the meeting noted that he had been asked by shareholders to defer the following resolutions to that General meeting in the New Year:
4
To re-appoint PKF Littlejohn LLP as auditors
5
To authorise the directors to fix the remuneration of the auditors
6
Authorise the Directors to allot Relevant Securities up to an aggregate nominal amount of £150,000
7
Subject to the passing of resolution 6, authorise the Directors to allot equity securities for cash or sell any equity securities of the company held as treasury shares
Accordingly, these were not put to the meeting on 30 December 2025 but will be taken at a General Meeting in the New Year
Full details of the resolutions can be found at -
https://wsg-corporate.com/wp-content/uploads/2025-12-02-Notice-Form-of-Proxy.pdf
For further information please contact:
Westminster Group Plc
Media enquiries via Walbrook PR
Rt. Hon. Sir Tony Baldry - Chairman
Peter Fowler - Chief Executive Officer
Mark Hughes - Chief Financial Officer
Strand Hanson Limited (Financial & Nominated Adviser and Broker)
James Harris
020 7409 3494
Ritchie Balmer
Richard Johnson
Walbrook (Investor Relations)
Tom Cooper
020 7933 8780
Joe Walker
Nick Rome
Westminster@walbrookpr.com
Notes:
Westminster Group plc is a specialist security and services group operating worldwide via an extensive international network of agents and offices in over 50 countries.
Westminster's principal activity is the design, supply and ongoing support of advanced technology security solutions, encompassing a wide range of surveillance, detection (including Fever Detection), tracking and interception technologies and the provision of long-term managed services contracts such as the management and running of complete security services and solutions in airports, ports and other such facilities together with the provision of manpower, consultancy and training services. The majority of its customer base, by value, comprises governments and government agencies, non-governmental organisations (NGO's) and blue-chip commercial organisations.
The Westminster Group Foundation is part of the Group's Corporate Social Responsibility activities.
www.wg-foundation.org
The Foundation's goal is to support the communities in which the Group operates by working with local partners and other established charities to provide goods or services for the relief of poverty and the advancement of education and healthcare particularly in the developing world.
The Westminster Group Foundation is a Charitable Incorporated Organisation, CIO, registered with the Charities Commission number 1158653.

latest.title: Accounts Timetable & Upcoming Trading Suspension
latest.content:
30 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 0321N
Westminster Group PLC
30 December 2025
30 December 2025
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION AS DEFINED IN ARTICLE 7 OF THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION NO. 596/2014 ("MAR") WHICH IS PART OF UK LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018, AS AMENDED. UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT, THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN
Westminster Group Plc
('Westminster', the 'Group' or the 'Company')
Annual Accounts Timetable and Upcoming Trading Suspension
Westminster announces that, due to timing constraints, the Company's Audited Annual Report and Accounts for the Year Ended 30 June 2025 (the "2025 Annual Accounts") will not be finalised by 31 December 2025 and therefore in accordance with the AIM Rule 19, trading in the Company's ordinary shares on AIM will be temporarily suspended from 7.30am on 2 January 2026.
As already notified in the AGM notice sent to shareholders on Thursday 4 December 2025, a separate general meeting will be held in the New Year with a resolution for the approval of the Report and Accounts. The Company will apply for the trading suspension to be lifted following publication of the 2025 Annual Accounts, which is expected early in the new year.
Updates on Various Financing initiatives
The Company advises of advanced discussions with a strategic investor with substantial business activities in Africa and the Middle East who has expressed an indicative interest in making a significant investment in Westminster and to collaborate on business opportunities. The Board anticipates the regional expertise that the potential investor has to be of considerable benefit as opportunities within the territories continue to develop.
In addition, the Company advises that it is in the final stages of negotiating a significant offshore banking facility for project financing.
Further updates will be made as and when appropriate.
​
For further information please contact:
Westminster Group Plc
Media enquiries via Walbrook PR

Nick Rome
Westminster@walbrookpr.com
Notes:
The Westminster Group Plc is a global integrated security services group, delivering niche security solutions and long-term managed services to high growth and emerging markets around the world, with a particular focus on long-term recurring revenue business.
Westminster's principal activity is the design, supply and ongoing support of advanced technology security solutions, encompassing a wide range of surveillance, detection, tracking and interception technologies and the provision of long-term managed services contracts such as the management and running of complete security services and solutions in airports, ports and other such facilities together with the provision of manpower, consultancy and training services. The majority of its customer base, by value, comprises governments and government agencies, non-governmental organisations (NGOs) and blue-chip commercial organisations.
The Westminster Group Foundation is part of the Group's Corporate Social Responsibility activities.
www.wg-foundation.org
The Foundation's goal is to support the communities in which the Group operates by working with local partners and other established charities to provide goods or services for the relief of poverty and the advancement of education and healthcare particularly in the developing world.
The Westminster Group Foundation is a Charitable Incorporated Organisation, CIO, registered with the Charities Commission number 1158653.

latest.content:

Westminster Group PLC
10 December 2025
10 December 2025
Westminster Group Plc
('Westminster', the 'Group' or the 'Company')
Posting of Notice of AGM
Westminster Group Plc (AIM: WSG), a leading supplier of managed services and technology-based security solutions worldwide, is pleased to confirm that it posted its Notice of AGM and Form of Proxy to shareholders on Thursday 4 December 2025 and posted a copy to its website at
https://wsg-corporate.com/wp-content/uploads/2025-12-02-Notice-Form-of-Proxy.pdf
. The AGM will be held in Westminster House, Banbury at 10am on the 30th December 2025.

company_30s.what_they_do: WSG is a specialist services provider that delivers engineering, security and support solutions to the public and private sectors. Its core offerings include on‑site technical support, managed security services and bespoke project management for infrastructure and government contracts.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company operates in a niche that is increasingly important as governments and large enterprises invest in cyber‑defence, infrastructure resilience and digital transformation. Its contracts with the Ministry of Defence and other public bodies give it a defensible moat against larger competitors.
company_30s.current_state: WSG is still in a survival mode. It has recently secured a handful of new contracts and a credit facility, but it is grappling with delayed accounts, an auditor shortfall and a temporary trading suspension. The share price is trading at the lowest point of its recent history, reflecting a deep capitulation.

rns_narrative: WSG’s recent announcements show a company trying to stabilize after a prolonged period of uncertainty. The AGM and accounts releases highlight governance delays and the need for a new auditor, while the credit facility and new contracts signal that the business is still generating revenue.

Two main themes emerge: 1) **Operational recovery** – new defence contracts, a credit line and a cyber‑tech division suggest that the company is still building revenue streams; 2) **Financial & regulatory turbulence** – delayed accounts, trading suspension and auditor issues create a cloud of uncertainty.
These signals hint that the firm is at a turning point: if the auditor sign‑off and the trading suspension lift, the market may finally see the underlying business model.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet uncertainty with a flicker of cautious optimism
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of posts praise the new cyber division and the credit facility, suggesting that some traders see a potential turnaround.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most commentary focuses on the auditor delay, trading suspension and the risk of delisting, painting a picture of a company still in crisis.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from outright skepticism (early 2025) to a more mixed view after the credit line was announced, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public interest spikes, which means any catalyst could generate a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The auditor’s final sign‑off and the lifting of the trading suspension are the most immediate catalysts that could unlock liquidity.
A formal announcement of the new cyber‑defence division’s first project win would demonstrate that the expansion strategy is working.
Positive market reaction to the credit facility’s terms (e.g., low interest, flexible maturity) could lift investor confidence.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next 2–3 months are: (1) the release of the final audited accounts; (2) any change in the trading status on the London Stock Exchange; (3) a spike in volume above the 20‑day average that coincides with a positive announcement.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early stages of a deep crash recovery. The APEX compression score suggests that the price is still far from a breakout point, so the market is likely still waiting for a clear catalyst before it will resume upward momentum.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: WSG sits at an 86% drop from its peak – a classic deep‑crash scenario that historically delivers strong rebounds. The recent contract wins and credit line show that the business model remains viable. If the company recovers to roughly 70% of its former valuation, the upside is about 200% from the current price, which far outweighs the 86% risk of further decline.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the auditor delay extends or the trading suspension is renewed, the company could face a delisting scenario. Additionally, the new cyber division has not yet proven its revenue potential, and the credit facility may be a temporary stop‑gap rather than a long‑term solution.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 86% of the peak (the current price). Potential gain: 200% if the price rebounds to 70% of the peak. The risk‑reward ratio is roughly 1:2.3, favouring a contrarian long stance.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the last support level (~0.02p) with at least a 10% volume increase. Size the position so that a 10% move against you would trigger a stop at 10% below the entry. Use a trailing stop to lock in gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position (or a protective put) and wait for the auditor sign‑off and the lifting of the trading suspension. A volume spike of 30% over the 20‑day average after a positive announcement would be a clear entry signal.
playbook.if_passing: If the trading suspension remains in place beyond the next RNS deadline or if the auditor refuses to sign off, consider cutting the position to avoid a potential delisting.

ai_insights.bottom_line: WSG is a deep‑crashed, 86%‑down play that could rebound sharply once the auditor issue clears and the trading suspension lifts, offering a 2:1 upside‑to‑risk asymmetry that rewards contrarian patience.
